steps.title: Use context to define the blank
steps.content: Tom walks out, looks at the fence, and immediately "all gladness left him and a deep melancholy settled down upon his spirit" — caused by seeing 30 yards of nine-foot board fence. The word "surveyed" describes what Tom did with the fence that triggered this despair. The word must mean something like looked at carefully or took in the full extent of.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Examined': Means to look at something closely and carefully. Tom visually took in the full size of the fence, which caused his despair. This fits the context of careful visual assessment. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Adjusted': Means to change or alter something. Tom did nothing to alter the fence; he only looked at it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Organized': Means to arrange in order. There is no rearranging of the fence in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Complimented': Means to express praise. Tom's reaction is despair, not admiration — the exact opposite of a compliment. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
